Published as a book in 1949 by University of Illinois Press with co-author Warren Weaver, separate from the original 1948 journal article in Bell System Technical Journal. Confirmed via Wikipedia, Amazon, and academic references
Consistent with task scoring system where Shannon book is worth fewer points (15) than the three conference papers (25 each)

privileged_info_summary: The task involves reclassifying four real academic papers in a Zotero library. 'Attention Is All You Need' (Vaswani et al., 2017, NeurIPS) should be reclassified as conferencePaper. 'Deep Residual Learning for Image Recognition' (He et al., 2016, CVPR) should be conferencePaper. 'ImageNet Classification with Deep Convolutional Neural Networks' (Krizhevsky et al., 2012, NIPS) should be conferencePaper. 'The Mathematical Theory of Communication' (Shannon & Weaver, 1949, published by University of Illinois Press) should be reclassified as book. All papers are real, published works with correct publication venue classifications. The task uses a 100-point scoring system: 25 points each for the three conference papers, 15 points for the Shannon book, and 10 points for preventing collateral damage (not modifying other items). Pass threshold is 60 points.
